authority_origin: Generative allocation and relayout events supply names; this rule only records them.
normal_behavior: Distinguish physical root rho, whole-root layout epoch lambda, and footprint-local state generation epsilon.
recoverable_behavior: Name construction has no recoverable effect.
abandonment_behavior: A dropped descriptive name grants no release or access.
binding_and_invalidators: Names bind exact allocation, layout, and local-state lineages; release or the matching generation change invalidates dependents.
disposition: No owner is carried.
checking: Nominal identity equality and freshness checks only.
erasure: All names erase.
native_need: All fourteen operations use stable physical identity.
deletion_witness: Without separate rho, lambda, and epsilon, logical handles can re-root storage or disjoint allocator subowners invalidate one another.
hostile_negative: Treating a machine counter or address as a fresh generative name is rejected.
falsifier: A wrapped integer revives authority or an unrelated local mutation invalidates a disjoint subowner.

authority_origin: None; place construction is descriptive elimination of an existing name.
normal_behavior: Construct root, declared field, checked index, or checked byte range descriptors.
recoverable_behavior: Failed arithmetic or containment returns no place.
abandonment_behavior: A dropped place carries no cleanup.
binding_and_invalidators: Every place binds rho, lambda, exact interval, and alignment; relayout or release invalidates it.
disposition: No owner is carried.
checking: Checked integer arithmetic followed by canonical interval normalization.
erasure: Place descriptors erase.
native_need: All four projects require exact suballocation places.
deletion_witness: Without places, safe code needs eager initialization, copies, per-element roots, backpointers, or per-use dynamic lookup.
hostile_negative: An address, page number, index, or handle cannot substitute for a place.
falsifier: A wrong-root or out-of-range descriptor is accepted without the checked derivation.

authority_origin: None; split and join establish geometry only.
normal_behavior: Split an exact interval product and rejoin pairwise-disjoint exact siblings.
recoverable_behavior: Unknown overlap or incomplete cover rejects without changing authority.
abandonment_behavior: Descriptive footprints carry no cleanup.
binding_and_invalidators: Footprints bind one rho and lambda plus canonical sorted intervals; relayout and release invalidate them.
disposition: No owner is carried.
checking: Sort interval endpoints, check overflow, disjointness, and exact cover in O(A log A).
erasure: Footprints erase.
native_need: Sparse slots, allocator blocks, page ranges, and active prefixes need exact disjointness.
deletion_witness: Without it, code pays coarse borrowing, copies, bitmaps, runtime alias tables, or unsafe overlap.
hostile_negative: A writer proposition that two dynamic ranges are disjoint is rejected.
falsifier: Unknown overlap is accepted or exact join omits bytes.

authority_origin: No new root or release authority; the existing whole-root lineage is transferred to a fresh lambda.
normal_behavior: Relayout only a reunited whole root with no loans, subowners, observations, progress, or dispositions.
recoverable_behavior: Failure before commit returns the predecessor whole-root state.
abandonment_behavior: Abandonment cannot release the root or expose a successor layout.
binding_and_invalidators: Consumes every lambda-bound descriptor, certificate, and fact; produces a fresh nonwrapping lambda.
disposition: Release remains escrowed.
checking: Exact whole-cover and empty-obligation checks followed by closed-layout validation.
erasure: Only the program's native resize or relayout event remains.
native_need: Root replacement and true whole-root layout changes only.
deletion_witness: Without it, resize either preserves stale places or requires runtime version tables and wrapper roots.
hostile_negative: Advancing lambda for an ordinary local mutation is rejected.
falsifier: A stale descriptor survives relayout or a disjoint local mutation advances the whole-root epoch.

authority_origin: Known allocation, checked byte initialization, closed built-in PlainData adoption, checked owner installation, or a linear predecessor supplies every leaf; this rule only seals the complete partition.
normal_behavior: Seal an exact cover whose leaves are Vacant, Bytes, Live, Transit, or Disposition; adoption accepts only the closed drop-free scalar, finite-enum, array, and product grammar.
recoverable_behavior: Incomplete, overlapping, misaligned, invalid-discriminant, or non-PlainData adoption returns all inputs unchanged.
abandonment_behavior: An abandoned seal has no successor authority; input lineage remains accounted by the enclosing path.
binding_and_invalidators: Each leaf binds rho, lambda, epsilon, exact footprint, and its fixed kind; overlapping writes or generation changes invalidate it.
disposition: Transit and Disposition leaves retain their exact close obligation.
checking: Canonical footprint cover, built-in layout/validity checks, and exact affine multiset equality; source cannot implement a validity trait.
erasure: Leaf kinds and generations erase.
native_need: Uninitialized capacity, initialized pages, live payloads, and partial states occur across the corpus.
deletion_witness: Without the leaf distinction, code must initialize, tag, scan, box, or permit uninitialized typed reads.
hostile_negative: Physical zero bits, a validator, pointer bits, external handle bytes, or writer validity code cannot produce Vacant, Bytes, or Live.
falsifier: A typed read is accepted from Vacant, adoption creates a resource owner, or one owner appears in two leaves.

authority_origin: Detach is the sole Subowner producer and consumes the matching parent leaf into Out; product, sum, span, split, and join only preserve lineage.
normal_behavior: Build closed products, closed sums, symbolic spans, and exact parent-frame/subowner pairs.
recoverable_behavior: Failed detach or join returns the complete predecessor.
abandonment_behavior: A live subowner may be abandoned only without releasing or reusing its parent root.
binding_and_invalidators: Parent and child retain rho and lambda; each fragment has an independent epsilon and exact Out identifier.
disposition: Join requires the matching frame Out and subowner; no orphan can release the root.
checking: Finite descriptor traversal and affine multiset equality; spans are checked symbolically, not unrolled.
erasure: Composition and Out identifiers erase.
native_need: All projects need products or spans; allocator blocks and page pins need fragment owners.
deletion_witness: Without it, code pays per-element roots, backpointers, wrapper allocation, scans, or premature whole-root release.
hostile_negative: Detaching while retaining the parent leaf or joining a different subowner is rejected.
falsifier: A root releases while an Out fragment remains or one fragment appears twice.

authority_origin: Initial sealing from a fully known partition or fixed complete plain-data adoption is the sole origin; later states consume the current classifier. RoleView is scoped elimination only.
normal_behavior: Maintain a finite role table selected by whitelisted native metadata and checked affine place mapping; selection retains the borrowed classifier lineage while creating only a scoped view.
recoverable_behavior: Decoder failure returns no RoleView and preserves the certificate.
abandonment_behavior: Dropping a certificate cannot release its root or convert its leaves.
binding_and_invalidators: Binds rho, lambda, epsilon, metadata footprint, payload map, phase, and exact metadata/payload invalidators.
disposition: Owner-bearing arms remain inside the affine certificate until a K3 transition or structural close.
checking: Whitelist AST validation plus finite-total role table and canonical map checks; no source call, loop, recursion, proof, or solver.
erasure: Classifier, phase, and RoleView erase; native metadata remains.
native_need: Sparse control/payload, page roles, and checked byte-page roles need metadata-selected maintained authority.
deletion_witness: Without it, representations need eager initialization, liveness bitmaps, persistent tags, scans, or unsafe metadata authority.
hostile_negative: A raw FULL byte, non-null link, page number, or validator result cannot mint RoleView.
falsifier: Metadata alone authorizes a live payload or a raw metadata write preserves the old certificate.

authority_origin: Known-empty sealing, fixed complete adoption, and linear push consuming one exact node are the only origins; selection, pop, and splice consume or borrow the current strict certificate.
normal_behavior: Maintain Nil or Node(place, child, rest) over unique disjoint leaves, with stored addresses only selecting certified children.
recoverable_behavior: Failed selection or empty pop preserves the prior chain.
abandonment_behavior: An abandoned chain cannot release its root; its structural disposition remains owed.
binding_and_invalidators: Binds rho, lambda, finite partition bound, checked place map, node shape, and next-field invalidators.
disposition: Pop returns the exact node and tail; structured close removes each obligation before its action.
checking: Validate one finite inductive schema and local push/pop equations; never traverse or unroll the runtime chain.
erasure: Strict certificates erase; native head and next fields remain.
native_need: Intrusive free chains, overflow ownership, and bounded detached chains need strict linear structure.
deletion_witness: Without it, safe code needs per-node roots, tags, bitmaps, backpointers, scans, or address-forged ownership.
hostile_negative: An arbitrary non-null head or duplicate node cannot produce a chain certificate.
falsifier: A copied address yields FreeNode authority or a cycle passes affine accounting.

authority_origin: Only an existing current live leaf or scoped RoleView produces a loan.
normal_behavior: Create a shared or unique scoped loan with exact root, range, state generation, and invalidators.
recoverable_behavior: Failed scope or compatibility check creates no loan.
abandonment_behavior: A loan cannot be abandoned into a longer scope or outlive the root.
binding_and_invalidators: Binds rho, lambda, epsilon, interval, mode, region, and every incompatible transition.
disposition: Scope end returns the escrowed incompatible authority to the parent state.
checking: Lexical region and affine CFG checks plus exact overlap testing.
erasure: Loans and source maps erase.
native_need: Zero-copy lookup, mutation, page parsing, and protected loads need root-exact access.
deletion_witness: Without it, code copies, boxes, uses coarse whole-root access, performs runtime checks, or accepts stale/wrong-root references.
hostile_negative: A place, address, guard, or metadata result alone cannot produce a loan.
falsifier: A loan escapes its state generation or survives an overlapping transition.

authority_origin: Known stable sealing is the sole initial origin; focus, progress, repair, poison, and stable successors are linear transfers.
normal_behavior: Represent Stable, exclusive Focus, structural Progress, Repair, and Poison with exact carried owners and obligations.
recoverable_behavior: Every recoverable arm names the exact predecessor, successor, repair, or poison resources.
abort_behavior: Abort has no successor and promises no cleanup.
abandonment_behavior: Returning abandonment cannot discard Transit or Disposition and cannot regain Stable.
binding_and_invalidators: Control state binds all affected roots, generations, cursor measure, partial events, and invalidators.
disposition: Only sealed resume, repair, poison, or structural disposition is available outside Stable.
checking: Finite outcome table plus exact authority-multiset equations and a fixed local decreasing measure.
erasure: Control tags erase unless the source representation already stores them.
native_need: Rehash, allocator collection, page balance, rollback, and bounded collection need partial progress.
deletion_witness: Without it, code needs snapshots, inverse closures, persistent protocol tags, mandatory local rollback, or exposes invalid intermediate state.
hostile_negative: A callback result or writer predicate cannot close Stable or erase an outstanding owner.
falsifier: A partial failure returns Stable or an obligation disappears on a returning path.

authority_origin: Reusable sealing requires shareable captures; Once sealing consumes the exact environment and existing obligations but creates no captured authority.
normal_behavior: Seal a reusable callable or affine Once<W,A,Sigma> with fixed effects, capture provenance, gate, and finite sink signature set.
recoverable_behavior: Failed inline or boxed packing returns the original environment; boxed allocation failure is explicit.
abandonment_behavior: Normal scope exit with an outstanding Once is rejected; abort need not invoke it.
binding_and_invalidators: Every capture binds exact roots, generations, ranges, thread rule, effects, and gate invalidators.
disposition: Once normal flow only transfers or consume-before-invokes; reusable captures retain their declared disposition.
checking: Structural capture/effect checking, finite signature membership, inline size/alignment test, and affine CFG accounting.
erasure: Static signatures and capture authority erase; only the source-selected call pointer and environment words or explicit box remain.
native_need: Crossbeam uses an inline heterogeneous once action; mimalloc separately uses an existing reusable callback.
deletion_witness: Without Once, the native route needs per-type queues, tags, boxes, refcounts, a second dynamic layer, or unsafe raw environments.
hostile_negative: A copyable address in place of a retired/disposition token or a second invocation is rejected.
falsifier: A Once is copied, invoked before its gate, or gains a hidden allocation or destructor pointer.

authority_origin: A domain originates only by sealing a complete fixed registry and ordering profile; ingress lineage originates only in checked publication and this rule merely seals its complete structural set.
normal_behavior: Seal finite lane roles, registry coverage, a complete Open-or-Closed ingress product/sum/span/strict source, and fixed ordering profile for later admission and observer validation.
recoverable_behavior: Incomplete registry, incomplete ingress cover, or profile failure returns every predecessor unchanged.
abandonment_behavior: A domain with open ingress, outstanding observations, or retired roots cannot release its registry.
binding_and_invalidators: Binds generative domain identity, registry roots, lane generations, exact target ingress slots, ordering profile, and structural invalidators.
disposition: Domain disposition requires every ingress closed, every lane inactive, no retired rights, and reunited registry roots.
checking: Finite role/profile validation and exact structural cover; registry and ingress lengths are symbolic folds, never writer reachability proofs.
erasure: Domain, ingress, and lane certificates erase; native registry, pointer slots, and gate state remain.
native_need: Allocator abandoned-page readers and epoch participants require one generic observer closure schema.
deletion_witness: Without it, release needs locks, per-load/per-object records, a policy-specific form, arbitrary unreachability, or arbitrary quiescence.
hostile_negative: A guard, epoch integer, successful CAS alone, address, policy name, partial ingress set, or absent participant scan cannot produce a closed domain state.
falsifier: An unlisted tail or auxiliary ingress remains open while the target becomes Retired.

authority_origin: Projection from an accepted K1-K3 judgment is the only fact origin.
normal_behavior: Project exactly Bounds, Alignment, LiveRole, SameRoot, Disjoint, CurrentGeneration, ProtocolState, Publication, or NonInterference.
recoverable_behavior: Failed projection produces no fact and retains runtime checks.
abandonment_behavior: Dropping a fact changes no program semantics.
binding_and_invalidators: The checker derives the complete dependency and invalidator set; matching lambda or epsilon change, scope end, interference, or release invalidates it, and source cannot add or omit invalidators.
disposition: Facts carry no owner or cleanup.
checking: Closed producer table and dependency-set inclusion only.
erasure: Facts erase; facts-off retains checks and semantics.
native_need: All projects can reuse accepted safety proofs as optimization facts.
deletion_witness: Without facts, repeated bounds and alias checks may remain; widening them creates stale optimizer authority.
hostile_negative: A writer assertion, writer invalidator list, unlisted fact kind, or stale fact cannot produce safety authority or check elision.
falsifier: A fact survives an invalidator, appears in an authority-origin premise, or facts-off changes semantics.

authority_origin: Checked construction, installation, adoption, and predecessor transitions establish value lineage; every other output is a transfer.
normal_behavior: Perform fixed initialize, take, replace-return-old, swap, overlap-checked relocate, destroy, byte overwrite, or reclassify traffic with one logical commit.
recoverable_behavior: Precommit failure returns every offered owner and predecessor; partial completion names exact successor obligations.
abandonment_behavior: Returning abandonment may not discard an owner, Transit, or release escrow.
binding_and_invalidators: Every operand and result retains rho, lambda, epsilon, interval, type/layout, and exact invalidators.
disposition: Every branch consumes, returns, moves, destroys, or escrows each owner exactly once.
checking: Opcode-specific affine multiset equations and checked place-map validation; no general relation inference.
erasure: Authority annotations erase; each opcode lowers to its exact native owner traffic.
native_need: The frozen routes require materially distinct initialize, take, replace, swap, relocate, destroy, and reclassify traffic.
deletion_witness: Deleting a traffic form forces an extra move, copy, temporary owner, allocation, overlap restriction, or project special case.
hostile_negative: A branch duplicates, loses, reconstructs, or restores an owner without its predecessor.
falsifier: Generated owner traffic differs from the declared opcode or a failure arm has the wrong multiset.

authority_origin: K2-LOAN is the only loan origin; this step begins, reborrows, and ends its fixed scope.
normal_behavior: Begin or end an exact shared/unique loan and return escrowed authority at scope end.
recoverable_behavior: Compatibility failure creates no loan.
abandonment_behavior: A loan cannot be leaked into a usable successor beyond its region.
binding_and_invalidators: Uses exact root, generations, interval, mode, and invalidators from the sealed source.
disposition: Scope end accounts every borrowed capability; it never destroys the underlying owner.
checking: Affine/scoped CFG dataflow plus interval conflict checks.
erasure: Borrow transitions erase.
native_need: All fourteen operations require zero-copy exact-root access.
deletion_witness: Without fixed borrowing, safe code copies, serializes unrelated places, or pays runtime borrow tables.
hostile_negative: A RoleView after its classifier mutation or a guard without observation cannot borrow payload.
falsifier: A stale, overlapping, or wrong-root loan is accepted.

authority_origin: K2-CONTROL supplies the initial sealed state; all K3 results are lineage-preserving transfers.
normal_behavior: Open focus, advance one structural item, and select exact success, recoverable, repair, poison, or terminal outcomes.
recoverable_behavior: Each returning arm exposes only its enumerated resource multiset.
abandonment_behavior: No returning abandonment may regain Stable or erase carried obligations.
binding_and_invalidators: Binds exact roots, generations, measure, prior events, and branch invalidators.
disposition: Success closes all obligations; repair and poison retain restricted executable disposition.
checking: Finite outcome lookup, local measure decrease, and exact multiset equality.
erasure: Progress state erases except native cursor and protocol state already present.
native_need: Rehash, allocator lifecycle, page algorithms, rollback, and collection require partial progress.
deletion_witness: Without it, code needs snapshots, inverse closures, persistent tags, unsafe stable exposure, or container-specific cleanup.
hostile_negative: A wildcard error arm, arbitrary rollback body, or callback boolean cannot choose authority.
falsifier: A rare path loses an owner or makes Stable before its obligations are empty.

authority_origin: A disposition originates only by converting an existing affine owner, partial outcome, detached source, or retired right to one fixed terminal action.
normal_behavior: Remove one obligation before fixed destroy, release, stable transfer, or admitted once invocation; structural folds consume a closed source measure.
recoverable_behavior: Failure retains the current obligation or returns an exact smaller source plus remaining action.
abandonment_behavior: Returning abandonment cannot silently discard the target or release its root.
binding_and_invalidators: Source, action, target root, generation, cutoff, and invalidators remain exact across transfer.
disposition: Every normal step strictly reduces a closed span, chain, child, product, or active-prefix measure.
checking: Constructor-specific fold equation and exact terminal-action whitelist.
erasure: Disposition and measure certificates erase; source iteration and terminal actions are exactly native.
native_need: Partial rehash, allocator chains, page pins, and deferred active prefixes need executable cleanup.
deletion_witness: Without it, code leaks, scans capacity, duplicates destruction, adds one finalizer per container, or accepts arbitrary cleanup code.
hostile_negative: A writer cleanup closure, nondecreasing returning step, or Retired without Quiescent is rejected.
falsifier: An owner disappears, a target releases twice, or an ineligible target is destroyed.

authority_origin: K2-CALLABLE is the only callable seal origin; invocation produces no new captured authority.
normal_behavior: Pack, transfer, enqueue, or consume-before-invoke an admitted callable; Once removes the slot before the existing indirect call.
recoverable_behavior: Packing or full-sink failure returns the unchanged environment or package; callable return follows its exact effect row.
abort_behavior: Package is consumed before a trap or divergent call; abort has no cleanup successor.
abandonment_behavior: Normal exit with outstanding Once is rejected.
binding_and_invalidators: Binds exact environment provenance, effects, target roots, thread rule, gate, and sink signature.
disposition: Normal return must consume or transfer every captured obligation.
checking: Finite signature membership, affine CFG flow, and direct effect-row checking.
erasure: Only the native call pointer, inline data, or explicit existing box remains.
native_need: Crossbeam requires one existing indirect once call; mimalloc needs a distinct reusable leaf.
deletion_witness: Without it, heterogeneous actions require tags, allocation, refcount, extra indirection, per-type containers, or unsafe raw bytes.
hostile_negative: Invoking before quiescence, copying Once, or moving thread-affine destruction is rejected.
falsifier: A second invocation is reachable or lowering adds runtime metadata/events.

authority_origin: Successful exact publication is the sole custody/share/initial-ingress origin; later atomic events linearly transform only their bound slot, and failed events preserve offered authority.
normal_behavior: Perform a fixed atomic event with exact success/failure custody, ordering, publication, and one slot-bound Open-to-Closed or ingress-move result.
recoverable_behavior: Failure and retry retain every offered owner and ingress state and expose observed native bits only.
abandonment_behavior: Abandonment cannot treat a failed event as a transfer or replay a successful event.
binding_and_invalidators: Binds target root, atomic slot, complete ingress-set identity, offered owner, old/new values, generation, ordering, and interference invalidators.
disposition: PublishedCustody remains affine; shared publication retains one release escrow; closing one slot does not close another.
checking: Opcode and target-profile memory-order table plus exact branch multiset and nominal ingress-state equations.
erasure: Only the selected native atomic or fence remains.
native_need: Remote free and Crossbeam publication/unlink require exact native custody events.
deletion_witness: Without it, ownership moves before CAS, extra locks/events appear, or atomics become unsafe assertions.
hostile_negative: A failed CAS loses its offered owner, a head CAS silently closes a tail ingress, or a relaxed event claims stronger ordering.
falsifier: Authority transfers on the wrong arm, one event is replayed, an unlisted ingress closes, or lowering strengthens the protected path.

authority_origin: Begin-observe events alone create Observation; retirement consumes PublishedCustody plus the complete all-Closed ingress set; OBS-CLOSE alone creates Quiescent.
normal_behavior: Begin/end observation, eliminate a complete ClosedIngress set into one Retired right, validate complete lanes strictly beyond cutoff, and combine Retired with matching Quiescent for terminal disposition.
recoverable_behavior: Open ingress, active old lane, incomplete registry, concurrent structural change, or stalled observer returns block/retry with no retirement or quiescence credit.
abandonment_behavior: A stalled observation blocks release; abandonment never fabricates ingress closure or observer exit.
binding_and_invalidators: Binds domain, target root, complete ingress identity, cutoff, lane generation, registration events, ordering profile, and ABA-invalidating root identity.
disposition: Each target keeps one affine PublishedCustody or Retired right; Quiescent owns no target and cannot release without it.
checking: Finite ClosedIngress and OBS-CLOSE premise checks plus one structural fold step; no per-load event, solver, reachability proof, or policy callback.
erasure: Ingress, observation, and cutoff proofs erase; only native pointer slots, registration, gate, scan, epoch, atomic, and queue state remain.
native_need: Mimalloc abandoned-page validation and Crossbeam participant epochs instantiate the same closure theorem.
deletion_witness: Without it, code needs global locks, per-load/per-object records, project policy forms, arbitrary reachability/invariants, or premature release.
hostile_negative: A guard, copied epoch, successful CAS alone, partial ingress/registry scan, unsafe defer promise, or wrapped label cannot produce Retired or Quiescent.
falsifier: An open ingress or pre-cutoff observer remains while target disposition becomes executable.

authority_origin: Exact allocation or acquisition creates root, release, and vacant bytes; successful full read or a sealed-profile fully initialized short-read result creates Bytes; no high-level state originates here.
normal_behavior: Execute one sealed low-level allocation, open/acquire, filesize, access, range read/write, truncate/resize, sync, lock/unlock, close/delete, target, or individually admitted ABI event.
recoverable_behavior: A failed read returns no readable byte authority unless its profile initializes the entire requested buffer; failed write, truncate, sync, delete, or lock transition returns either its reported effect or a closed conservative Indeterminate effect, and K3-CONTROL retains Repair or selects Poison.
abandonment_behavior: Cancellation preserves only the exact or conservative event state and cannot restore unstated bytes, durability, existence, or lock state.
binding_and_invalidators: Binds sealed platform row, ABI, handles, roots, requested range, success extent, declared indeterminate effect set, ordering, error class, and every invalidator.
disposition: Handles, buffers, scratch roots, and recoverable event state remain owned until transferred or closed; a consumed close may end software access while recording a possible external leak.
checking: Finite compiler-owned profile/opcode table lookup plus exact authority equations; no high-level pager, WAL, callback, reinitializer, provider catch-all, or project recognition.
erasure: Only the source-selected low-level event remains.
native_need: Allocation/release, SQLite open/filesize/access/I/O/truncate/sync/locks/close/delete, target atomics, barriers, and individually sealed ABI rows are irreducible external semantics.
deletion_witness: Without these leaves external effects are unavailable; widening them hides pager, recovery, allocator, container, or reclamation operations.
hostile_negative: A generic read error yields Bytes, unknown failed write is unchanged, open provider control yields authority, or a row returns Stable, Quiescent, RepairComplete, role, edge, or unstated fact.
falsifier: A hidden pager/WAL operation closes the route or an unreported partial effect is accepted as exact success.

authority_origin: Projection from a successfully checked judgment is the sole producer class.
normal_behavior: Create or invalidate one fixed-schema optimizer fact with exact dependencies.
recoverable_behavior: Projection failure leaves the runtime check and produces no fact.
abandonment_behavior: Dropping a fact changes no safety or runtime semantics.
binding_and_invalidators: Binds exact roots, generations, scopes, events, and invalidators.
disposition: Facts carry no disposition and cannot feed authority.
checking: Finite projection table and dependency-set comparison.
erasure: All fact state erases; facts-off is semantically identical.
native_need: Repeated bounds, layout, publication, and alias facts can justify proof-elision.
deletion_witness: Without it, checks remain; widening it allows stale or writer-defined optimization assumptions.
hostile_negative: A fact is used to mint liveness, ownership, observation, retirement, quiescence, or release.
falsifier: Facts-on changes authority acceptance or a stale fact removes a check.

authority_origin: Exact allocation/acquisition is the only release-authority origin; this rule only consumes it.
normal_behavior: Release one reunited whole root after every subowner, loan, observation, progress, retired right, and disposition has ended.
recoverable_behavior: Failed release event retains the exact restricted successor required by its leaf contract.
abandonment_behavior: Abandonment leaks but cannot expose reusable or addressable successor authority.
binding_and_invalidators: Consumes rho, lambda, whole partition, release escrow, and all root-bound facts; reuse starts a fresh rho.
disposition: Release is terminal; no place or footprint survives.
checking: Exact whole-cover, empty-obligation, and terminal leaf checks.
erasure: Only the native release event remains.
native_need: Every allocation-backed route eventually needs exact safe root release.
deletion_witness: Without it, code leaks or permits release through an unrelated wrapper/address.
hostile_negative: Release with an Out fragment, loan, observation, retired target, or partial repair is rejected.
falsifier: A released root remains addressable or reuse revives an old authority.

authority_origin: No authority origin; acyclic composition of K1-NAMES, K1-PLACE, K1-PARTITION, and K2-LEAF.
normal_behavior: Derive the former physical-place analytical job from the normalized core.
recoverable_behavior: Component failures fail closed.
abandonment_behavior: No independent abandonment rule.
binding_and_invalidators: Inherits exact rho, lambda, epsilon, intervals, and invalidators.
disposition: Inherits component disposition.
checking: Deterministic expansion only.
erasure: Fully erases.
native_need: All four projects.
deletion_witness: Deleting the derivation only removes a name; deleting a dependency has the dependency's witness.
hostile_negative: No extra primitive or syntax is admitted.
falsifier: Expansion differs from the named dependency list.

authority_origin: No authority origin; acyclic composition of K1-PARTITION, K2-LEAF, K2-COMPOSE, K2-CLASSIFY, and K2-STRICT.
normal_behavior: Derive structural-live layouts from closed partition constructors.
recoverable_behavior: Component failures fail closed.
abandonment_behavior: No independent abandonment rule.
binding_and_invalidators: Inherits all root, generation, range, and metadata invalidators.
disposition: Inherits structural disposition.
checking: Deterministic expansion only.
erasure: Fully erases except native representation state.
native_need: All four projects.
deletion_witness: Deleting a dependency forces its recorded tag, bitmap, scan, copy, or unsafe alternative.
hostile_negative: No writer predicate or topology family is admitted.
falsifier: Expansion contains a project identity or open grammar.

authority_origin: No authority origin; acyclic composition of K3-OWNER, K3-ATOMIC, K3-CONTROL, and K2-COMPOSE.
normal_behavior: Derive fixed owner transitions and their exact outcomes.
recoverable_behavior: Component outcomes preserve exact owners.
abandonment_behavior: No independent abandonment rule.
binding_and_invalidators: Inherits operand roots, generations, ranges, and events.
disposition: Inherits exact owner disposition.
checking: Deterministic expansion only.
erasure: Lowers to component opcodes.
native_need: Owner-moving operations across the corpus.
deletion_witness: Deleting a component forces extra owner traffic or loses an exact atomic transfer.
hostile_negative: No project-named traffic form is admitted.
falsifier: Expansion adds a move, copy, allocation, or hidden owner.

authority_origin: No authority origin; acyclic composition of K2-CONTROL, K3-CONTROL, and K3-DISPOSE.
normal_behavior: Derive focus, monotone progress, repair, and poison.
recoverable_behavior: Every outcome is exact.
abandonment_behavior: No independent abandonment rule.
binding_and_invalidators: Inherits control-state roots, measure, events, and invalidators.
disposition: Inherits restricted repair/poison disposition.
checking: Deterministic expansion only.
erasure: Erases except native cursor/state.
native_need: Partial-progress routes in every project family.
deletion_witness: Deleting control or disposition forces snapshots, inverse cleanup, persistent tags, or unsafe stable exposure.
hostile_negative: No arbitrary protocol or rollback program is admitted.
falsifier: An intermediate state escapes as Stable.

authority_origin: No authority origin; acyclic composition of K1-PLACE, K1-PARTITION, K2-LOAN, and K3-BORROW.
normal_behavior: Derive exact physical-root footprints for every borrow and source map.
recoverable_behavior: Failed derivations produce no borrow.
abandonment_behavior: No independent abandonment rule.
binding_and_invalidators: Inherits exact rho, lambda, epsilon, ranges, modes, and invalidators.
disposition: Scope end closes all loans.
checking: Deterministic expansion only.
erasure: Fully erases.
native_need: All fourteen operations.
deletion_witness: Deleting a dependency forces copies, dynamic checks, coarse aliasing, backpointers, or wrong-root acceptance.
hostile_negative: No wrapper, handle, address, or logical key can re-root a borrow.
falsifier: A result names the wrong physical allocation.

authority_origin: No authority origin; acyclic composition of K2-COMPOSE, K2-CLASSIFY, K2-STRICT, K2-CALLABLE, K3-DISPOSE, K3-CALLABLE, and K3-RELEASE.
normal_behavior: Derive executable disposition as a closed structural fold with fixed leaves.
recoverable_behavior: Each failure retains an exact smaller or unchanged source.
abandonment_behavior: No independent abandonment rule.
binding_and_invalidators: Inherits source roots, action identities, measures, gates, and invalidators.
disposition: Every returning step removes one obligation before its terminal action.
checking: Deterministic expansion only.
erasure: Only native iteration and terminal actions remain.
native_need: Rehash, allocator chains, page resources, and deferred active prefixes.
deletion_witness: Deleting a dependency forces leaks, capacity scans, container finalizers, tags, boxes, or arbitrary cleanup.
hostile_negative: No writer cleanup program or hidden dynamic action set is admitted.
falsifier: A returning step fails to reduce or duplicates a terminal action.

authority_origin: No authority origin; acyclic composition of K2-FACT and K3-FACT.
normal_behavior: Derive invalidatable facts only as read-only projections.
recoverable_behavior: Failure retains checks.
abandonment_behavior: No independent abandonment rule.
binding_and_invalidators: Inherits exact dependency and invalidator sets.
disposition: No owner disposition.
checking: Deterministic expansion only.
erasure: Fully erases.
native_need: All project families may use proof-elision.
deletion_witness: Deleting it retains checks; widening it risks stale optimizer authority.
hostile_negative: Facts cannot feed safety authority.
falsifier: Facts-on changes accepted authority or facts-off changes semantics.

authority_origin: No authority origin; acyclic composition of K2-OBSERVER, K3-ATOMIC, K3-OBSERVE, K3-DISPOSE, and K3-RELEASE.
normal_behavior: Derive atomic custody, observation, retirement, quiescence, and final disposition without selecting a reclamation policy.
recoverable_behavior: Incomplete or stalled validation fails closed.
abandonment_behavior: No independent abandonment rule.
binding_and_invalidators: Inherits domain, root, cutoff, lane, ordering, admission, and invalidator bindings.
disposition: Retired target plus valid Quiescent is required for disposition.
checking: Deterministic expansion only.
erasure: Only native atomics, registry/gate state, scans, and release remain.
native_need: Mimalloc and Crossbeam concurrent routes.
deletion_witness: Deleting a dependency forces locks, per-load/per-object records, a policy form, arbitrary invariants, or unsafe release.
hostile_negative: No guard, epoch integer, partial scan, or writer assertion can produce quiescence.
falsifier: A pre-cutoff observer survives executable target disposition.
